ops.title: Parallel Claude sessions in yasyf's repos ship releases mid-flight — verify latest tag AND the registry (PyPI/GitHub) immediately before choosing a version, and tag immediately after the release commit lands
ops.body:
Both cc-notes and captain-hook (colocated jj repos, see [[jj-colocated-use-jj-not-git-stash]]) host concurrent Claude sessions that commit to `main@origin` and cut releases while another session's work is in flight. On 2026-07-12 a planned capt-hook "9.5.0" release was renumbered twice: 9.5.0 had shipped before execution started, then another session tagged and published 9.6.0 (browser-delegation nudge) between our change-set landing on main and our `git tag` — the tag call failed with "already exists" and our CHANGELOG entries were sitting in the wrong version section on pushed main.

**Why:** version numbers picked at plan time go stale by execution time; a shared working tree also means `@` can silently gain or lose a parent when the sibling session commits (check `jj log -r @-`, not a bookmark-inclusive revset — `main@origin` showing in the log does NOT mean it's your parent).

**How to apply:** right before tagging, re-check `git tag --sort=-creatordate | head` AND the registry (`curl pypi.org/pypi/<pkg>/json` / `gh release list`); if taken, renumber forward with a fix-forward commit (move CHANGELOG entries to the new section, sweep semantic "since/pre-X" references in code comments, test names, and docs), never rewrite pushed history; then tag in the same breath as the push to shrink the race window. Expect mid-run CHANGELOG conflicts on rebase — resolve by taking the superset side.
